VERDICT_TEXT: Darktrace is financially strong — taken private by Thoma Bravo for $5.3B in October 2024, with ~$782M revenue and 89% gross margins — but the company culture is the most significant concern. Glassdoor shows only a 49% recommend rate, a 3.0/5 culture score, and repeated accounts of toxic management and poor HR practices. Leadership instability is also a real flag: the company has had three CEOs since September 2024, with Ed Jennings only stepping in as CEO in March 2026. The Amsterdam office is active and hybrid (2 days/week in office), but IND Kennismigrant sponsorship status could not be confirmed from public records — verify directly with HR before progressing.
VERDICT_FLAGS: 🚨 Culture: 49% recommend rate, toxic management reports ⚠️ Leadership: 3 CEOs since Sep 2024 ⚠️ Visa: IND Kennismigrant status unconfirmed ✓ Financials: $5.3B PE-backed, $782M revenue, 89% gross margin ✓ Product: 4.3–4.5/5 on G2 and Capterra
LAYOFFS_CONTENT:
Thoma Bravo stated at the time of the October 2024 acquisition that it did not plan any material restructuring or material headcount reduction, and would focus only on reducing non-critical administrative costs.

However, multiple employee reviews from late 2024 and early 2025 on Glassdoor describe layoffs following the acquisition — with accounts of sudden terminations conducted in 5-minute calls. Headcount data from Unify shows approximately 324 recent departures alongside ~200 new hires, suggesting meaningful attrition. No entry was found on layoffs.fyi for Darktrace.

The Netherlands office was not specifically mentioned in layoff reports.
LAYOFFS_SOURCES:
• Darktrace layoff reviews — Glassdoor
  • Thoma Bravo completes acquisition of Darktrace — Thoma Bravo
  • Employee headcount data — Unify
FUNDING_TIMELINE:
Series E2020 · $50M · Vitruvian Partners, KKR, 1011 Ventures · Valuation $1.65B
IPO — London Stock ExchangeApril 2021 · £165M raised · Valuation ~£2.5B
Take-Private by Thoma BravoOctober 2024 · $5.3B · Delisted from FTSE100/LSE
FINANCIAL_SIGNALS:
Darktrace reported revenue of approximately £625M (~$782M) for FY2024 (year ending June 2024), with gross margins of ~89% and positive EBITDA. Net ARR retention rose to 106.6% and the customer base grew 10.6% year-on-year to 9,735 customers. Operating cash flow was $110M (TTM).

The company is now wholly owned by Thoma Bravo following the $5.3B take-private in October 2024. No public financial reporting is required going forward. Prior to delisting, FY2023 showed net income of $59M on $545M revenue.

In January 2023, short seller Quintessential Capital Management published a report alleging accounting irregularities and channel-stuffing. An independent review by EY (July 2023) found only a small number of immaterial errors — no evidence of fraud.
FINANCIAL_PLAIN_ENGLISH: Darktrace makes money selling AI cybersecurity software, and it's genuinely profitable with nearly 90 cents of gross profit on every dollar of revenue. It was bought outright by a large US private equity firm (Thoma Bravo) for $5.3 billion in late 2024, so it's no longer on the stock market. A short seller accused it of cooking the books in 2023, but an independent audit found nothing material — that chapter appears closed.

LEADERSHIP_CURRENT:
As of April 2026, the executive leadership team is:

  • Ed Jennings — President & CEO (appointed March 2026)
  • Jack Stockdale OBE FREng — Chief Technology Officer (co-founder)
  • Suman Raju — Chief Financial Officer
  • Hein Hellemons — Chief Revenue Officer
  • David Smith — Chief People Officer
  • Nicole Eagan — Co-founder and Strategic Advisor
  • Phil Pearson — Chief Strategy Officer
  • Matthew North — EVP, EMEA and APJ Sales
LEADERSHIP_DEPARTURES:
• Poppy Gustafsson (CEO, co-founder) — stepped down September 2024, weeks before Thoma Bravo acquisition completed. Now UK Minister of State for Investment.
  • Jill Popelka (CEO) — appointed September 2024, stepped down January 2026, reportedly under pressure from Thoma Bravo. Charles Goodman (board chairman) served as interim CEO.
  • Ed Jennings — installed as permanent CEO March 2026.

Three CEOs in approximately 18 months is a material red flag for stability at the top.

LEGAL_CONTENT:
No active lawsuits, class actions, FTC, SEC, or EEOC enforcement actions were found against Darktrace as of April 2026. No Dutch regulatory actions (AP, ACM, or court proceedings) were identified.

Short-seller controversy (2023): Quintessential Capital Management published a report in January 2023 alleging accounting irregularities, channel-stuffing, and connections to individuals linked to fraud. Darktrace commissioned an independent review by EY, which in July 2023 found only a small number of immaterial errors — no evidence of fraud. The matter was referred to the UK FCA and FRC, and no further action has been reported.

Mike Lynch connection: Darktrace's founding investor Mike Lynch faced US fraud charges related to the Autonomy/HP sale (a separate legal matter). Lynch was acquitted in June 2024 and died in August 2024. This has no direct bearing on Darktrace's operations or legal standing.
LEGAL_SOURCES:
• Darktrace under attack from short sellers — CNBC
  • EY review finds no fraud — UK Tech News
  • Darktrace cleared by EY — AccountingWEB
GLASSDOOR_RATINGS:
Overall rating3.3 / 5 (1,298 reviews)
Recommend to a friend49%
Work-life balance3.5 / 5
Culture & values3.0 / 5
Career opportunities3.1 / 5
Compensation & benefits3.1 / 5
CULTURE_THEMES:
Positives: Reviewers consistently praise innovative technology, strong product, good colleagues at the individual contributor level, and exposure to cutting-edge AI cybersecurity.

Negatives: Recurring and consistent themes across hundreds of reviews include: toxic management culture, inexperienced and unqualified managers, no performance review process, no salary increases, high pressure and unrealistic targets, and very high turnover (average tenure reportedly less than one year). Multiple reviews mention that the company hires recent graduates at low base salaries.

Post-Thoma Bravo acquisition reviews describe increased pressure and a sense of instability, with 3 CEO changes in 18 months amplifying concerns.
CULTURE_COMMUNITY:
• "Toxic culture. Avoid at all costs." — Glassdoor review
  • "3 CEOs in 3 years, no performance reviews, no salary increases, multiple resignations in GTM teams." — Glassdoor review (2025)
  • "Since Thoma Bravo acquired, there have been mass layoffs and additional pressure." — Glassdoor review
  • "Layoffs are done in 5-minute calls." — Glassdoor review
  • "A luxurious brand but employees are treated as slaves." — Glassdoor review

RTO_OFFICIAL: Darktrace's Amsterdam office operates on a hybrid model with a minimum of 2 days per week in office. The company also maintains a European R&D centre in The Hague (from its acquisition of Cybersprint in 2022). FlexJobs lists Darktrace as offering hybrid and fully remote positions, though engineering roles are more likely to require office attendance.
RTO_CHANGES: No formal RTO policy announcement or change was found specific to Darktrace in 2024 or 2025. The hybrid-minimum policy for Amsterdam appears to have been consistent.
RTO_SENTIMENT: Employee reviews do not flag the work location policy as a major concern. The more pressing issues cited are management quality and culture rather than office attendance requirements.
RTO_SOURCES:
• Darktrace remote/hybrid jobs — FlexJobs
  • Amsterdam job listing noting hybrid policy — LinkedIn
COMP_SALARY:
No specific salary data was found for a Data Engineer role at Darktrace in the Netherlands. As a benchmark, software engineering roles in the Netherlands at Darktrace's peer companies typically range from €69,000–€121,000 per year (Levels.fyi Netherlands data). Glassdoor rates Darktrace's compensation at 3.1/5, suggesting it may track below market.

Amsterdam sales roles at Darktrace were found at €40,000–€45,000 base with uncapped commission — consistent with reviews noting the company recruits graduates at low base salaries.
COMP_EQUITY: No equity or stock option information specific to Netherlands-based Darktrace roles was found. As a private company (since October 2024), any equity would be in the form of options or profit-sharing rather than publicly traded shares — the liquidity timeline and terms are unclear.
COMP_BENEFITS: Reviews mention standard benefits including health insurance and pension contributions, but no standout benefits programme was identified. The 3.1/5 compensation and benefits score on Glassdoor is below average for a company of this size and maturity.
COMP_SOURCES:
• Darktrace salaries — Levels.fyi
  • Darktrace salary data — Glassdoor
  • Darktrace salary — PayScale
INTERVIEW_CONTENT:
Based on 196 Glassdoor submissions, the Darktrace interview process takes an average of 23 days end-to-end. Difficulty is rated 2.69/5 (moderate). Only 48.5% of candidates rate the experience positively — below average for a tech company.

The typical structure involves: recruiter screening, technical discussion, scenario or case-based assessment, culture fit, and a final manager interview. For technical roles this may include a take-home or live coding component.

Negative feedback highlights poor communication, ghosting after later rounds, and disorganised scheduling. No Data Engineer-specific interview reports were found.
INTERVIEW_SOURCES: • Darktrace interview questions and experience — Glassdoor
VISA_CONTENT:
The relevant Dutch immigration route for a Data Engineer role is the IND Kennismigrant (Highly Skilled Migrant) permit. To use this route, Darktrace must be registered as an erkend referent (recognised sponsor) with the Dutch IND. Recognised sponsors can process Kennismigrant applications in as little as 2 weeks.

Darktrace has an active Amsterdam office and posts roles there, which suggests they may hold this status — however, Darktrace's presence on the IND public register of recognised sponsors could not be confirmed during this research. The IND register requires a direct search at ind.nl.

Recommendation: Before progressing, confirm directly with Darktrace's HR team whether they are an IND-recognised sponsor and whether they actively support Kennismigrant applications for Data Engineer roles.
VISA_SOURCES:
• IND public register of recognised sponsors
  • Hiring a highly skilled migrant — Government.nl
PRODUCT_CONTENT:
Darktrace's core product is an AI-powered cybersecurity platform that uses unsupervised machine learning to detect threats across network, email, cloud, and OT environments. Key modules include Darktrace/Detect, Darktrace/Respond (Antigena), Darktrace/Email, and Darktrace/OT.

G2: 4.3/5 from 59 verified reviews. Praised for autonomous response, self-learning AI, and ease of deployment. Critiques: alert fatigue, tuning complexity, and pricing.

Capterra: 4.5/5 from 19 reviews. Customers highlight the EMAIL module as best-in-class and praise customer support. Concerns: cost and multi-site deployment complexity.

Customer base grew 10.6% YoY to 9,735 customers as of FY2024. January 2025 acquisition of Cado Security adds cloud forensics capabilities.
PRODUCT_SOURCES:
• Darktrace product reviews — G2
  • Darktrace reviews — Capterra
PROFILE_SUMMARY:
Darktrace is a British AI cybersecurity company founded in 2013 in Cambridge, UK, by mathematicians and intelligence experts including Poppy Gustafsson, Jack Stockdale, Nicole Eagan, Dave Palmer, and Emily Orton. It was backed from inception by Invoke Capital, Mike Lynch's venture fund.

The company applies unsupervised machine learning to detect and autonomously respond to cyber threats in real time. It operates across network, email, cloud, endpoint, and operational technology (OT) security. As of 2023 it had approximately 2,269 employees across 100+ countries.

In the Netherlands, Darktrace has an Amsterdam office (sales/go-to-market) and a The Hague R&D centre, the latter inherited from its €47.5M acquisition of Dutch company Cybersprint in 2022.
PROFILE_MILESTONES:
• 2013 — Founded in Cambridge, UK, backed by Invoke Capital (Mike Lynch)
  • 2018 — Achieved unicorn status
  • 2019 — CEO Poppy Gustafsson and CTO Jack Stockdale awarded OBEs
  • 2021 — IPO on London Stock Exchange, valuation ~£2.5B
  • 2022 — Acquired Dutch company Cybersprint for €47.5M
  • 2023 — Short-seller attack by Quintessential Capital; EY review clears company
  • October 2024 — Taken private by Thoma Bravo for $5.3B; delisted from LSE
  • January 2025 — Acquired Cado Security (cloud forensics)
  • March 2026 — Ed Jennings appointed CEO

FOUNDER_CONTENT:
Poppy Gustafsson (co-founder, former CEO) — studied mathematics at Sheffield, qualified as a chartered accountant at Deloitte, then worked as corporate controller at Mike Lynch's Autonomy before co-founding Darktrace in 2013. She served as CFO, then COO, then CEO from 2016 to September 2024. Awarded an OBE in 2019. In October 2024, she was appointed UK Minister of State for Investment and elevated to the House of Lords. No personal legal controversies.

Jack Stockdale OBE FREng (co-founder, CTO) — still in role. Awarded OBE in 2019. No controversies found.

Mike Lynch (founding investor via Invoke Capital) — known as the "British Bill Gates." Faced long-running US fraud charges related to the $11.7B sale of Autonomy to Hewlett-Packard. Was acquitted in June 2024. Died in August 2024 when his superyacht sank off Sicily. His legal history was separate from Darktrace operations and Darktrace was never charged.

Nicole Eagan (co-founder) — now Strategic Advisor at Darktrace. No controversies.
